title: digitalis subalpina
intro: Digitalis subalpina, also known as Alpine Foxglove, is a flowering plant species within the Digitalis genus, closely related to the common Foxglove. The plant contains cardiac glycosides, particularly digoxin and digitoxin, which have been used for centuries to treat various heart conditions due to their medicinal properties. The health benefits of Digitalis subalpina include its ability to improve heart function, reduce symptoms of heart failure, and alleviate arrhythmias, making it a valuable herbal remedy for cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Digitalis subalpina typically involve drying the leaves and flowers to create a tincture or tea, which can be consumed under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
